questions.label: Scenario 1: Scheduling a Meeting
questions.scenario: You're trying to schedule a meeting with a global team member. What's the best approach?

options.label: A
options.text: Pick a time that works for you.

options.label: B
options.text: Intentionally find the schedule overlap rather than forcing your hours.

options.label: C
options.text: Schedule it for late night for one team, early morning for another.

questions.label: Scenario 2: Disconnected Team Member
questions.scenario: An employee expresses that they feel disconnected from the team. What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Tell them to try harder to connect.

options.label: B
options.text: Reassure them, then investigate why they feel disconnected, and work to bond with them.

options.label: C
options.text: Ignore it, hoping it will pass.

questions.label: Scenario 3: Communication Style
questions.scenario: How should you adapt to someone's communication style?

options.label: B
options.text: Expect them to adapt to your style.

options.label: C
options.text: Document their preferred communication style instead of guessing.

questions.label: Scenario 4: Meeting Priorities
questions.scenario: Are live meetings the only priority for global teams?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes, live meetings are always best.

options.label: B
options.text: No, treat asynchronous norms as just as important as live meetings.

options.label: C
options.text: Only schedule live meetings if absolutely necessary.
